body.title: [sprint] Audits are self-attested — require run-record artifacts consumed by the next pipeline step
body.description:
intention: I intend that 'the audit ran' is a checkable artifact, not a free claim.

gate-attack-report.20260707.md headline + G3/G4: every audit gate can be skipped or 'run inline' with no evidence — audit_telemetry is unshipped (T-audit-telemetry-shape, open since 2026-04-29) and nothing CONSUMES a run record. Shape: (1) ship the minimal run-record emit (level, ran_at, checks fired/passed, verdict) per T-audit-telemetry-shape's schema into the store or ~/.unseen_university telemetry home; (2) TEETH — the consuming step checks for it: /sorted refuses to file tickets without an audit-ticket run-record for the batch; /sprint-ticket refuses first edit without a precode run-record; close path refuses without the smell record when smell was due. Warn-only checks (intention field) become blocking once records exist.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/audit_telemetry.py (new, per T-audit-telemetry-shape), skills/sorted/SKILL.md, skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md, skills/audit-*/SKILL.md (emit step), devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (close-path check)

**Design rules:** external state; log every state change; no escape hatches (CP6)

**Scope boundary:** run-record emission + consumption checks ONLY; no change to any audit's checks themselves; extends (does not duplicate) T-audit-telemetry-shape — reference it and close both together if its schema is adopted unchanged.

**Test plan:** hermetic — run a filing without the record, assert refusal; with the record, assert pass; record schema round-trips.

**Completion criteria:** filing/sprint/close each refuse when their required run-record is absent; a session transcript can no longer claim an unrun audit.

**Validation:** Observable via refusal log lines (log.warning("gate: missing run-record <level> for <artifact>")).

audit-emphasis: needs-deep-smell

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.gate: T-audit-telemetry-flatfile-repoint (run-record substrate points at retired Postgres palace; teeth must gate on a flat-file record)
body.intention: I intend that 'the audit ran' is a checkable artifact, not a free claim.
